title: Catalyst Center Alert to Splunk On-Call/SOAR Routing
dataSources: Primary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:issue`. from the `issue` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per active issue per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `priority` (string), `category` (string), `name` (string), `deviceName` (string), `status` (string), `issueId` (string), `siteId` (string), `lastOccurenceTime` (epoch milliseconds). (P1/P2 issues). Secondary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:event:notification`. via Catalyst Center webhook → Splunk HEC (push model, real-time). Configure under Platform > Developer Toolkit > Event Notifications. Volume: Variable; event-driven. Depends on subscribed event types and network activity.. RBAC: SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE (for configuring event subscriptions in Catalyst Center). Key fields: `eventType` (string), `eventCategory` (string), `eventSeverity` (integer), `description` (string), `timestamp` (epoch milliseconds), `domain` (string), `tenantId` (string), `source` (string). (critical events). Both feed into the alert action pipeline for Splunk On-Call/SOAR routing.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + Splunk On-Call (VictorOps) and/or Splunk SOAR (alert actions)
spl: index=catalyst ((sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" (priority="P1" OR priority="P2") status!="RESOLVED") OR (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:event:notification" eventSeverity<=2)) | eval alert_source=case(sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue","Assurance Issue: ".name, sourcetype="cisco:dnac:event:notification","Event: ".description, 1==1,"Unknown") | eval severity=case(priority="P1" OR eventSeverity=1,"critical", priority="P2" OR eventSeverity=2,"high", 1==1,"medium") | table _time alert_source severity deviceName siteId
description: Routes critical Catalyst Center alerts (P1/P2 issues and severity 1-2 events) to Splunk On-Call (VictorOps) or Splunk SOAR for automated incident creation and on-call escalation.
value: Manual alert triage introduces delay. Automated routing to On-Call/SOAR ensures critical network issues reach the right engineer within seconds.
implementation:
Save the SPL as an alert with the following alert actions:

**For Splunk On-Call (VictorOps):**
1. Install the Splunk On-Call (VictorOps) app for Splunk
2. Configure the alert action to create an incident with:
   - Routing key: `network-ops`
   - Message type: `CRITICAL` for P1/severity-1, `WARNING` for P2/severity-2
   - Entity ID: `catalyst-center-$deviceName$`

**For Splunk SOAR:**
1. Configure the HEC connection from Splunk to SOAR
2. Set the alert to trigger a SOAR playbook that:
   - Creates a ServiceNow incident
   - Pages the network on-call team
   - Runs initial diagnostics (ping, show commands via Catalyst Center Command Runner API)

Schedule the alert to run every 5 minutes with a 5-minute window.
visualization: Alert-driven: no primary chart; optional supporting table in a runbook dashboard showing last 20 routed events with dedup key on deviceName+issueId.

references.title: Catalyst Center Issues API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Lab or test device P1/P2 issue routed to the production on-call team.** A lab device generating a P1/P2 issue or severity 1-2 event notification triggers the SOAR/on-call routing, paging the production team unnecessarily. Distinguish by checking whether `deviceName` matches a lab naming convention or is in a non-production `siteId`. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup and filtering lab devices from the routing logic.

**Planned maintenance P1/P2 issue triggering SOAR playbook.** A device reload during a maintenance window generates a P1/P2 issue that triggers the automated response. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM change records. Suppress by integrating the `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup into the SOAR routing logic — suppress routing when the alert falls within an approved maintenance window.

**Duplicate routing from both issue and event notification matching the same incident.** The SPL matches both `cisco:dnac:issue` and `cisco:dnac:event:notification` sources. A single network event may trigger two SOAR actions. Distinguish by checking whether the `deviceName` and timestamp match across both sourcetypes. Suppress by deduplicating: group by `deviceName` and use a 15-minute deduplication window.

**SOAR playbook failing on enrichment step due to missing Catalyst Center API access.** The SOAR playbook may attempt to query the Catalyst Center API for enrichment and fail if the SOAR platform does not have API credentials configured. Distinguish by checking SOAR playbook execution logs for API errors. This is not a false positive — fix the SOAR-to-Catalyst Center connectivity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with issue and/or event notification data flowing to `index=catalyst`.
• Splunk SOAR (formerly Phantom) or Splunk On-Call (formerly VictorOps) integrated with the Splunk search head.
• Alert action configured: SOAR playbook trigger, Splunk On-Call webhook, or PagerDuty integration.
• Define escalation policies: which Catalyst Center event types and severities route to which teams.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC does not require additional data collection. It builds on existing Catalyst Center data (issues, event notifications) and routes alerts to external response platforms.

Routing decision points:
• **Severity-based routing:** P1/severity-1 → immediate page via Splunk On-Call/PagerDuty. P2/severity-2 → email + Slack. P3/P4 → dashboard only.
• **Category-based routing:** Network issues → NOC team. Security issues → SOC team. Compliance issues → compliance team.
• **Site-based routing:** Route to the team responsible for the affected site.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" priority IN ("P1","P2") status="ACTIVE") OR (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:event:notification" (eventSeverity=1 OR eventSeverity=2))
| eval source_type=if(sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue", "TA Poll", "Webhook")
| eval severity=coalesce(priority, "S".eventSeverity)
| stats count as event_count values(source_type) as detection_sources latest(description) as detail by deviceName, severity, category
| lookup catalyst_site_team_map siteId OUTPUT team_name, escalation_policy
| eval route_to=case(severity IN ("P1","S1"), "SOAR_CRITICAL", severity IN ("P2","S2"), "SOAR_HIGH", 1==1, "DASHBOARD_ONLY")
| where route_to!="DASHBOARD_ONLY"
| dedup deviceName sortby -_time
| table deviceName, severity, category, detail, team_name, route_to
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **Combined source filter:** Matches both TA-polled issues (P1/P2) and webhook event notifications (severity 1-2), providing comprehensive coverage regardless of the data path.
• **`coalesce(priority, "S".eventSeverity)`**: Normalizes the severity field across both sourcetypes — issues use "P1"/"P2", event notifications use numeric severity.
• **`lookup catalyst_site_team_map`**: Routes the alert to the appropriate team based on the affected site. Create this lookup with `siteId`, `team_name`, and `escalation_policy` columns.
• **`dedup deviceName`**: Prevents duplicate routing when both the issue and event notification for the same device match.
• **`route_to`**: Determines the alert action — SOAR_CRITICAL triggers immediate paging, SOAR_HIGH triggers email/Slack notification.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Test alert flow:** Create a test P1 issue in Catalyst Center (e.g., put a device in maintenance mode). Verify the alert flows through Splunk → SOAR/On-Call → team notification within 5 minutes.
• **Routing accuracy:** Verify the `catalyst_site_team_map` lookup routes alerts to the correct team for each site.
• **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Alert configuration:** Save as a scheduled search running every 5 minutes. Configure two alert actions: (1) SOAR playbook for CRITICAL routing, (2) email/Slack for HIGH routing.
• **SOAR playbook:** The playbook should: (a) acknowledge the alert, (b) query Catalyst Center API for device details, (c) create an ITSM ticket, (d) page the on-call engineer if P1.
• **Deduplication:** Throttle per `deviceName + severity` for 30 minutes to prevent alert storms.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Alerts not reaching SOAR:** Verify the Splunk-SOAR integration is configured. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "sendmodaction"` for SOAR action execution.
• **Duplicate routing:** Both issue and event notification may trigger for the same device. The `dedup deviceName` in the SPL should prevent this; verify it's working.
• **Lab devices triggering production alerts:** Add lab devices to a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup and filter them from the routing search.
• If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:issue`, check that the `issue` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We automatically send the most critical Catalyst Center alerts to your on-call team and incident management system so no one has to manually watch for problems. This helps your team act on facts instead of guesses.
